summary: Implement a nightly Azure Function (Python, Linux Consumption) that queries GPT-4o via TRAPI to retrieve the 1985 New York Yankees roster and persists it to an Azure Blob Storage container, using managed identity exclusively (no API keys). Infrastructure is provisioned via Bicep; CI/CD via GitHub Actions.
assumptions: TRAPI endpoint and managed-identity scope for GPT-4o are available and documented internally.
The target Azure subscription, resource group, and region are already allocated.
Python 3.11 is the runtime (aligned with existing infra memory: linuxFxVersion Python|3.11).
Blob container name is 'yankees-roster' (aligned with existing infra memory).
AzureWebJobsStorage uses identity-based connection (AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName, no listKeys).
RBAC roles required: Storage Blob Data Owner, Storage Queue Data Contributor on the storage account.
GitHub Actions is the CI/CD platform.
No VNet integration required (Consumption plan, public endpoints).
Application Insights will be used for observability.
The nightly schedule is midnight UTC (cron: 0 0 * * *).
The spec does not specify auto-approval of the task plan, so manual approval is required.
open_questions: What is the TRAPI endpoint URL and required OAuth scope for GPT-4o access?
Is the managed identity for the Function App pre-approved for TRAPI/GPT-4o access, or does it need to be granted?
What is the exact blob name/format for the stored roster (JSON, CSV, text)?
Are there retry or idempotency requirements if the nightly run fails mid-execution?
What alerting channels (email, Teams, PagerDuty) should be configured for failures?
Is there a required retention policy for blobs in the yankees-roster container?
What Azure region should resources be deployed to?
Is there a staging/pre-production environment, or only production?

waves.name: Foundation
waves.objective: Provision all required Azure infrastructure (storage, function app, RBAC, monitoring) via Bicep and establish the GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline skeleton.
entry_criteria: Azure subscription and resource group are available.
TRAPI/GPT-4o managed identity access is confirmed or unblocked.
Repository secrets and environments are configured in GitHub.
exit_criteria: Bicep templates deploy successfully to Azure with no errors.
Function App is reachable (placeholder function returns HTTP 200).
Storage account and blob container 'yankees-roster' exist.
Managed identity RBAC assignments are validated.
GitHub Actions CI pipeline executes lint and unit tests on every PR.
Review and approve task plan issue is closed.

waves.name: Core Build
waves.objective: Implement the nightly Azure Function that fetches the 1985 Yankees roster via TRAPI/GPT-4o and stores it to blob storage using managed identity.
entry_criteria: Foundation wave exit criteria are all met.
TRAPI endpoint URL and managed identity scope are confirmed.
Blob container and RBAC assignments are validated.
exit_criteria: Function executes end-to-end locally and in Azure (timer trigger fires, GPT-4o returns roster, blob is written).
Unit tests cover TRAPI client, blob writer, and orchestration logic with ≥80% coverage.
All PRs merged and approved by @copilot.

waves.name: Hardening
waves.objective: Add reliability, security controls, observability, and operational readiness to the deployed solution.
entry_criteria: Core Build wave exit criteria are all met.
Application Insights workspace exists and is linked to the Function App.
exit_criteria: Retry and exception handling verified via fault-injection tests.
No secrets or API keys present in code or config.
Application Insights alerts are active and tested.
Structured logging emits correct fields for all execution paths.
Security scan passes with no critical findings.

waves.name: Release Readiness
waves.objective: Validate the full solution end-to-end, complete documentation, and perform production rollout with post-deployment verification.
entry_criteria: Hardening wave exit criteria are all met.
Staging deployment is stable for at least one nightly run.
exit_criteria: At least one successful nightly execution confirmed in production logs.
All success criteria in spec.md are checked off.

tasks.title: Review and approve implementation task plan
tasks.description: Review this task plan for completeness, sequencing, and alignment with spec.md requirements. Resolve all open questions before approving. Once approved, close this issue to unblock all Foundation wave work.
outputs: Resolved open questions documented in issue comments
acceptance_criteria: All open questions are answered or explicitly deferred with documented rationale.
Task plan is reviewed and approved by the project owner (rciapala).
Issue is closed, unblocking Foundation wave tasks.
risks: Unresolved TRAPI endpoint or managed identity access could block Core Build wave.

tasks.title: Provision core Azure infrastructure via Bicep
tasks.description: Author or update Bicep modules under /infra to provision the Storage Account (with 'yankees-roster' blob container), Linux Consumption Function App (Python 3.11), Application Insights, and identity-based AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName connection. Tags: project=1985-NY-Yankees, owner=rciapala.
acceptance_criteria: Bicep templates deploy with `az deployment group create` without errors.
Storage Account exists with blob container 'yankees-roster'.
Function App is Linux Consumption with linuxFxVersion=Python|3.11.
AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName app setting is present; no storage connection string with account key.
Application Insights is linked via APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING app setting.
Resource tags project=1985-NY-Yankees and owner=rciapala are present on all resources.
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and merged.
risks: Identity-based storage requires preview features in some regions; verify regional availability.
Consumption plan cold starts may affect timer trigger reliability.

tasks.title: Configure RBAC assignments for managed identity
tasks.description: Add Bicep RBAC module to assign Storage Blob Data Owner and Storage Queue Data Contributor roles to the Function App system-assigned managed identity on the Storage Account. Also configure any TRAPI/GPT-4o managed identity role assignments once endpoint details are confirmed.
acceptance_criteria: Function App managed identity has Storage Blob Data Owner on the storage account.
Function App managed identity has Storage Queue Data Contributor on the storage account.
No storage account keys are used anywhere in configuration.
If TRAPI requires an RBAC assignment, it is provisioned and validated.
Validation: run `az role assignment list --assignee <principalId>` and confirm expected roles.
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and merged.
risks: TRAPI RBAC requirements are unknown; may require coordination with a separate team.
Propagation delay for RBAC assignments can cause transient 403 errors during first deploy.

tasks.title: Set up GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline
tasks.description: Create GitHub Actions workflows: (1) a CI workflow that runs lint (flake8/ruff) and unit tests (pytest) on every PR, and (2) a CD workflow that deploys the Function App via `func azure functionapp publish` or Azure Functions Action on merge to main. Use OIDC federated identity (no secrets) for Azure authentication.
outputs: OIDC federated credential configured in Azure
acceptance_criteria: CI workflow triggers on every PR and runs lint + unit tests; fails PR if tests fail.
CD workflow triggers on merge to main and deploys to the Function App successfully.
Azure authentication uses OIDC (azure/login action with client-id, tenant-id, subscription-id); no client secrets stored in GitHub.
Pipeline badge added to README.
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and merged.
risks: OIDC federated credential setup requires Azure AD app registration; may need admin approval.
First deployment may fail if INF-01 is not fully stable.

tasks.title: Implement TRAPI/GPT-4o client for roster retrieval
tasks.description: Implement a Python module (`trapi_client.py`) that authenticates to the TRAPI endpoint using `azure-identity` DefaultAzureCredential (no API keys), sends a prompt to GPT-4o requesting the 1985 NY Yankees roster, and returns a structured list of players. Include retry logic with exponential backoff.
inputs: TRAPI endpoint URL and OAuth scope (open question)
acceptance_criteria: Module authenticates using DefaultAzureCredential; no API keys or secrets in code or config.
Returns a list of player dicts (at minimum: name, position) for the 1985 Yankees.
Retries on transient HTTP 429/503 with exponential backoff (max 3 retries).
Unit tests mock DefaultAzureCredential and TRAPI HTTP responses; achieve ≥80% coverage of trapi_client.py.
All unit tests pass: `pytest tests/test_trapi_client.py -v` (link test results in PR).
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and merged.
risks: TRAPI endpoint details are an open question; implementation may be blocked.
GPT-4o response format may be non-deterministic; prompt engineering needed for consistent structured output.

tasks.title: Implement Azure Blob Storage writer module
tasks.description: Implement a Python module (`blob_writer.py`) that writes the player roster (as JSON) to the 'yankees-roster' blob container using `azure-storage-blob` with DefaultAzureCredential. Blob name should include a timestamp for auditability (e.g., `roster-YYYYMMDD.json`).
acceptance_criteria: Module authenticates using DefaultAzureCredential; no storage account keys in code or config.
Writes roster as UTF-8 JSON to 'yankees-roster' container with timestamped blob name.
Overwrites existing blob for the same date (idempotent writes).
Unit tests mock BlobServiceClient and validate correct container/blob name construction; achieve ≥80% coverage.
All unit tests pass: `pytest tests/test_blob_writer.py -v` (link test results in PR).
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and merged.
risks: RBAC propagation delay may cause 403 on first integration test run after INF-02 deploy.

tasks.title: Implement nightly Timer Trigger Azure Function
tasks.description: Implement the Azure Function (`function_app.py`) with a TimerTrigger cron schedule (0 0 * * * - midnight UTC) that orchestrates APP-01 (fetch roster) and APP-02 (store roster). Include structured logging via `logging` module with correlation to Application Insights.
inputs: Azure Functions Python v2 programming model docs
acceptance_criteria: Timer trigger cron expression is `0 0 * * *` (midnight UTC daily).
Function orchestrates TRAPI fetch and blob write in sequence; logs start, success, and failure events.
Exceptions are caught, logged with full stack trace, and re-raised to allow Azure Functions retry policy.
Unit tests mock trapi_client and blob_writer and verify orchestration logic; achieve ≥80% coverage.
All unit tests pass: `pytest tests/test_function_app.py -v` (link test results in PR).
Function deploys via CI-01 CD pipeline and appears as 'Running' in Azure portal.
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and merged.
risks: Timer trigger cold start on Consumption plan may cause first-run delays.
Midnight UTC schedule must account for daylight saving time implications if a local-time schedule is later required.

tasks.title: Configure Application Insights and alert rules
tasks.description: Add Bicep module for Application Insights workspace-based resource linked to the Function App. Create alert rules for: (1) function execution failures, (2) zero executions in a 25-hour window (missed nightly run). Configure action group for email/Teams notification.
acceptance_criteria: Application Insights workspace-based resource is deployed and linked to Function App via APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING.
Alert fires within 5 minutes of a simulated function failure (test by throwing exception in dev).
Alert fires if no successful timer executions are detected in a 25-hour window.
Action group routes alerts to at least one notification channel (email minimum).
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and merged.
risks: Alert channel (email/Teams) not specified in spec; must be resolved before this task can be completed.

tasks.title: Security review: no-API-key compliance and secret scanning
tasks.description: Perform a security review to verify no API keys, connection strings with keys, or secrets appear in code, configuration, or git history. Enable GitHub secret scanning and push protection on the repository. Validate all Azure connections use managed identity.
acceptance_criteria: GitHub secret scanning is enabled with push protection active on the repository.
No API keys, account keys, or connection strings with credentials found in code, config, or git history.
All Azure SDK clients use DefaultAzureCredential or ManagedIdentityCredential exclusively.
local.settings.json (if present) is in .gitignore and contains no real credentials.
Security scan (trufflehog or equivalent) passes with zero high/critical findings.
Findings report linked in PR; PR created with @copilot assigned for review and merged.
risks: Historical commits may contain accidentally committed secrets requiring git history rewrite.

tasks.title: Add retry and error handling hardening
tasks.description: Harden the function with explicit retry configuration in host.json (maxRetryCount, retryDelay), poison-message/dead-letter handling for persistent failures, and circuit-breaker pattern for TRAPI calls. Ensure the function is idempotent (same-day re-runs overwrite the blob without duplicating data).
acceptance_criteria: host.json configures fixedDelay retry with maxRetryCount=3 and retryDelay of 5 minutes.
Function is idempotent: running twice on the same day overwrites the blob without error.
TRAPI client stops retrying after circuit-breaker threshold (3 consecutive failures) and logs a critical alert.
Fault-injection unit tests verify retry and circuit-breaker behavior; all tests pass (link results in PR).
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and merged.
risks: Azure Functions Consumption plan retry policy behavior differs from Premium; validate on Consumption.

tasks.title: Add structured logging and distributed tracing
tasks.description: Ensure all function execution paths emit structured log entries (JSON format) with fields: run_id, trigger_time, player_count, blob_name, duration_ms, status (success/failure), error_message. Validate logs appear correctly in Application Insights Log Analytics.
outputs: Updated function_app.py and trapi_client.py with structured logging
acceptance_criteria: Every function execution emits a structured log entry with all required fields.
Logs are queryable in Application Insights using KQL (sample query provided in README).
End-to-end trace (trigger → TRAPI call → blob write) is visible as a single operation in Application Insights.
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and merged.
risks: Application Insights ingestion lag (up to 5 minutes) may slow debugging during hardening.

tasks.title: End-to-end integration test and production validation
tasks.description: Execute an end-to-end integration test in the Azure environment: manually trigger the function, verify the blob is written to 'yankees-roster', confirm all 40 players on the 1985 Yankees roster are present, and validate Application Insights logs show a successful run.
acceptance_criteria: Function executes successfully when triggered manually in Azure (exit code 0, no exceptions in logs).
Blob 'yankees-roster/roster-YYYYMMDD.json' is created with all expected 1985 Yankees players.
Player list is validated against a reference roster (minimum: correct count and key player names).
Application Insights shows a successful execution trace with no errors.
Test report linked in PR; PR created with @copilot assigned for review and merged.
risks: GPT-4o response non-determinism may return slightly different player lists on each run.
TRAPI rate limits may throttle test executions.

tasks.title: Write README and operational runbook
tasks.description: Write a README.md covering: project overview, local development setup, environment variables (no secrets), deployment instructions, and how to manually trigger the function. Write an ops runbook covering: how to check function execution history, how to re-run a failed execution, alert response procedures, and KQL queries for troubleshooting.
acceptance_criteria: README.md covers local setup, deployment, and manual trigger instructions.
Runbook documents alert response, re-run procedure, and at least 3 KQL diagnostic queries.
No secrets or real credentials appear in any documentation.
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and merged.
risks: Documentation may become stale if implementation details change during hardening.
